QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_4

question.text: During FY 2025-26, Ms. Priya has the following capital gains and losses: Long-Term Capital Gain (LTCG) from equity shares: Rs.3,00,000; Short-Term Capital Loss (STCL) from debt mutual funds: Rs.1,80,000; Long-Term Capital Loss (LTCL) from gold: Rs.2,50,000. What is the net capital gain taxable after intra-head set-off?

answer.text: Rs.3,00,000, because STCL and LTCL cannot be set off against equity LTCG
Nil, as total losses (Rs.4,30,000) exceed total gains (Rs.3,00,000)
Nil (LTCG Rs.3,00,000 minus STCL Rs.1,80,000 minus LTCL Rs.1,20,000), with Rs.1,30,000 LTCL carried forward
Rs.70,000, after setting off only LTCL Rs.2,50,000 against LTCG, with STCL carried forward separately

explanation.text: **The Logic:** STCL can be set off against ANY capital gain (STCG or LTCG). LTCL can ONLY be set off against LTCG. Optimal set-off: First set off STCL Rs.1,80,000 against LTCG Rs.3,00,000 (remaining LTCG = Rs.1,20,000). Then set off LTCL Rs.2,50,000 against remaining LTCG Rs.1,20,000. Net taxable = Nil. Unabsorbed LTCL = Rs.2,50,000 - Rs.1,20,000 = Rs.1,30,000 carried forward for 8 years. **The Trap:** Option D reverses the priority — setting off LTCL first is suboptimal, but the law doesn't mandate order. The key trap is Option A which wrongly suggests these losses cannot be set off against equity LTCG. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) tests capital gains set-off in mutual fund switch/redemption scenarios. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always set off STCL first against LTCG because STCL has wider flexibility — preserve LTCL for future LTCG only, but use STCL against any gain available now.
